problem_statement: Protect households from false data injection attacks (FDIA) in smart grids by providing an accessible, real-time anomaly detection system to prevent inflated bills, device damage, and privacy breaches.

market_information.market_size: India has over 90 million smart meters installed as of 2024; smart home market estimated at USD 7.3B by 2025.
target_segments: Tech-savvy urban households with high-speed internet and multiple smart devices
customer_needs: Protection against FDIA and voltage fluctuations
Willingness to pay 10-15% premium for advanced appliance protection
Preference for simplified dashboards and plug-and-play installation

market_opportunity.regulatory_drivers: National Cybersecurity Strategy 2023 and BIS IS 17855:2022 mandate grid protection
market_opportunity.cyberattack_trends: 61% YoY increase in power sector cyberattacks; FDIA attempts up 290% globally
market_opportunity.competitive_gap: Enterprise solutions cost ₹15L+; general cybersecurity lacks FDIA focus; no existing consumer-level FDIA protection

market_information.growth: Consumer cybersecurity market CAGR ~19.4%, current penetration <5%; rising renewable integration (175 GW by 2025)

technical_approach.solution_description: A device integrating smart voltage regulation with AI-powered FDIA detection using precision sensors and a deep LSTM neural network to monitor household voltage and current in real time.

architecture.local_analytics: LSTM-based engine (3 layers, 64 units each) comparing expected vs actual measurements using MSE analysis
architecture.distributed_intelligence: Optional network of devices sharing anonymized threat data to refine detection accuracy

technical_approach.validation: Prototype validated on UCI Household Electric Power Consumption dataset and testbed simulations achieving ~92% detection accuracy with <3% false positives

innovation: First consumer-level solution merging voltage stabilization with AI-driven FDIA detection, using a three-tier architecture combining edge monitoring, local LSTM analytics, and distributed intelligence for proactive anomaly detection beyond traditional voltage stabilizers.

consumer_benefits: Prevents energy fraud and inflated bills
Delivers actionable insights via an intuitive dashboard
impact.market_impact: Addresses a critical security gap in millions of smart homes defenseless against cyber tampering; supports national cybersecurity mandates; reduces appliance damage by up to 60%

feasibility.technical_feasibility: Prototype demonstrates high accuracy and low false positives; uses affordable consumer-grade hardware components

economic_feasibility.unit_pricing: Hardware priced ₹2,500–4,500/unit with 40–45% margin; premium units ₹4,500–7,000 with 50–55% margin
economic_feasibility.subscription_model: ₹99–299/month for advanced monitoring; enterprise contracts ₹2–5L per deployment
economic_feasibility.unit_economics: LTV:CAC ratio ~8:1; payback period 11–14 months; lifetime value >₹9,500
economic_feasibility.scalability: Aligned with growing consumer cybersecurity market and regulatory drivers
